name: Brattens Famous Clam Chowder
recipeInstructions:
1. Drain the juice from the clams and pour it over the onions, celery, and potatoes.
2. Add enough water to barely cover the ingredients.
3. Cook the vegetables until done.
4. In a separate pan, melt the butter.
5. Gradually add the flour and stir constantly until smooth.
6. Slowly incorporate the milk, stirring continuously until the mixture is smooth and thick.
7. Stir in the salt and pepper.
8. Add the white sauce, clams, and vinegar to the vegetable mixture.
9. Heat through until the chowder reaches the desired thickness.
10. Serve hot. Makes about 8 servings.
My variations: Add 3 cans of clams (and juice) instead of 2, also use about 4-6 cups of potatoes, 3 tablespoons of red wine vinegar. Comes from a restaurant that used to be located here in the valley. We would go there on special occasions.
Submitted by: Terrie Peterson
